title: Manfreda scabra
taxonomy: Manfreda scabra is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. It is further classified within the subfamily Agavoideae. As a member of this family, Manfreda scabra is related to other plants such as agaves and yuccas. Manfreda scabra is a species of the genus Manfreda, which belongs to the tribe Agavoideae. This classification helps understand the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Manfreda scabra is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant has been used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems.
